item.title: 2026 World Cup Deals Broken Down: Which Ones Are Worth Your Money?
item.dc:creator: CableTV.com
item.description: Don’t pay full price for soccer coverage. We break down the latest 2026 World Cup deals, including free trials, discounts, and bundles.
item.content:encoded:
With the 2026 FIFA World Cup right around the corner, it’s time to figure out how you’ll be tuning into the tournament. Whether you want to catch every single match or just make sure you have your preferred commentary team on lock, you’ll need to sort through the smattering of streaming promos available. Before we dig into deals, a word on languages. Broadcasts of the 2026 FIFA World Cup are split along language lines: FOX Sports (including FOX, FS1, and FOX One) carries only English-language coverage, while Telemundo Deportes (including Telemundo, Universo, and Peacock Premium) offers only Spanish-language coverage. I’ve been keeping a close eye on all the new discounts—for English and Spanish broadcasts alike—cropping up for the World Cup. Let me break down the best 2026 World Cup deals to help you decide which one’ll give you the most fútbol action for your buck, regardless of which language you speak.

YouTube TV World Cup deals: Best for Multilingual Viewing

YouTube TV is arguably the best overall option for World Cup viewing because it carries FOX, FS1, Telemundo, and Universo—giving you complete coverage of both English and Spanish broadcasts.

YouTube TV

Price: $82.99/mo. Channels: 100+

Currently, YouTube TV is offering a few deals to curb the pain of a high subscription cost:
  • 10-day free trial: YouTube TV always offers a free trial, but the length varies. As of June 10, 2026, they’re serving up a 10-day free trial that gives you over a week to test the interface risk-free. If you decide you like it, a current discount drops the price to $67.99/mo. for your first three months. That gives you a total of $45 off your bill before the regular price of $82.99/mo. kicks back in.
  • Sports Plan: If you only care about English broadcasts, you could opt for YouTube TV’s new Sports Plan. This tier features FOX and FS1 for a base price of $64.99/mo., but a current promotion lowers that cost to $54.99/mo. for a full 12 months.

Like I said, this is arguably the best option for hardcore World Cup watchers who want both English and Spanish-language broadcast coverage. However, if you only need one language, we’ve got some cheaper options for you below.

Want more TV sports coverage?

Subscribe to CableTV.com's email newsletter for the latest event-viewing guides, entertaining takes, and money-saving promotions.

By signing up, you agree to our Privacy Policy & Terms and Conditions.

FOX One World Cup deals: Most Affordable (English)

FOX One is a top pick for World Cup fans, with our Sports Editor, Taylor Kujawa, naming it the most affordable option for English-language broadcasts. Luckily, FOX One is also serving up some slick deals to make their soccer offerings even more affordable. Check ’em out:

FOX One

Price: $19.99/mo.

  • Buy 2 months, get 1 free: If you select this promo on the FOX One Subscribe page, you get three months of FOX One for $39.98. The World Cup lasts 39 days, so subscribers will need two months anyway, and FOX will want people to stick around longer. This offer ends July 19, the day of the World Cup Final.
  • 3-day free trial: FOX One previously offered a seven-day free trial, but now it’s just three days with its standard monthly plan. This might be ideal for fans who already get FOX over-the-air but need FS1 for the busy end of the Group Stage, when FOX and FS1 games air simultaneously—particularly Thursday, June 25, through Saturday, June 27. FOX One has a multiview option, making it the perfect setup for that period of the tournament.

Our verdict: If you’re an English-language World Cup viewer, FOX One is the streamer to get. All 104 matches—70 on FOX and 34 on FS1—will stream live and on demand in 4K on FOX One. Just be sure to snag one of the deals above when you sign up. Want even more FOX One deals? I’ll keep our dedicated FOX One deals guide up to date with promos during the World Cup and beyond.

Peacock World Cup deals: Most Affordable (Spanish)

Peacock is the choice for Spanish-speaking soccer fans this World Cup. With its premium plans that include every Telemundo and UNIVERSO broadcast, you’ll get all 104 World Cup games for almost half the cost of other streaming options. Peacock’s already affordable, but there are a few ways to cut back the costs even more:

Peacock Premium

Price: $10.99–$16.99/mo.

  • Discounted plans: Students, teachers, and military members can get Peacock Premium at a discounted price for 12 months, instead of the typical $10.99/mo. For students, the rate is $5.99/mo., while for teachers and military members, it’s $6.99.
  • No-cost subscriptions: You may already be eligible for free Peacock and just not know it. Walmart+ and Instacart+ members get Peacock Premium at no extra cost. Meanwhile, Xfinity Internet customers with an eligible plan can also receive a free Peacock Premium subscription.
  • Free matches: Peacock Select subscribers will get two key opening matches: Mexico vs. South Africa (June 11) and USA vs. Paraguay (June 12). However, apart from those matchups, subscribers need one of Peacock’s premium plans to enjoy the rest of the World Cup.

TL;DR: If you’re a Spanish-speaking soccer fan, Peacock is the streamer you’ll want for the World Cup.

Fubo World Cup deals

Fubo is a sports-centric live TV streamer (although not our favorite, that’ll be the next provider) that carries FOX and FS1 for English-language World Cup coverage, though it’s missing the Telemundo networks for this tournament.

Fubo

Price: $14.99–$103.99/mo. Channels: 26–296+

Fubo is slinging a few deals, though, so take a peek:

  • 5-day free trial: If you want to use Fubo to watch the Cup, take advantage of its free trial to get used to the interface.
  • $10 off: New subscribers can get a $10 discount the first month of the Sports + News plan (regularly $55.99/mo.), which includes FOX and FS1 to catch the matches in English.

TBH, we don’t recommend signing up for Fubo just to watch the Cup, as other providers in this guide offer the same thing for less (like FOX One).

DIRECTV World Cup deals

One perk of choosing DIRECTV as your World Cup streamer is its customizability. If you want to sign up for our pick for the Best Streaming Service for Sports, here are some cheaper ways to do it:

  • $30 off: New streaming customers get $30 off their first month, after a five-day free trial.
  • Language-specific Genre Packs: DIRECTV also offers a MySports Genre Pack that includes FOX and FS1 for $49.99/mo. for the first two months, which is a great pick for English-language viewers. The MiEspañol Genre Pack gets you Telemundo and Universo for $29.99/mo. for the first two months to watch the Cup en Español.

DIRECTV

Price: $84.99–$149.99/mo. (plus tax)

Channels: 90–185+

We’d recommend DIRECTV mainly if you’re a hardcore sports fan beyond the World Cup. As this page shows, there are cheaper options than DIRECTV if you only want to stream the Cup. But if you want top-tier, year-round sports coverage, we recommend giving DIRECTV a test drive. For more details, check out our dedicated guide to watching the World Cup on DIRECTV.

Tubi World Cup deals

The free streaming service Tubi is packed with World Cup content, as highlighted on our Tubi World Cup page. Here are Tubi’s top offers:

  • Free matches: While you can’t watch the entire tournament on the platform, fans can stream the two premier opening matches—Mexico vs. South Africa on June 11 and USA vs. Paraguay on June 12—completely free on the platform in English.
  • World Cup hub: Tubi’s FIFA World Cup FOX Hub page, easily accessible from its homescreen, will be updated throughout the Cup with replays, highlights, analysis, and more.

Although Fox Corporation-owned Tubi’s main goal is funneling folks over to FOX One to catch the bulk of the World Cup matches, we still think Tubi is great both for casual World Cup fans who care more about highlights than catching every match, as well as budget-conscious fans who don’t wanna open their wallets (and TBH, who can blame them).The World Cup ain’t cheap. Wanna know just how much Americans are willing to spend to catch the Cup? Check out our recent survey on how much it costs to watch the World Cup for the damage.

Which World Cup deal is the best?

On face value, any discount you can get on watching the World Cup seems great. But look closely—don’t get sucked into a multi-month subscription (cough, DIRECTV) when you only want a month-and-change’s worth of soccer content. We do think YouTube TV and DIRECTV offer tons of great World Cup content, but their price tags are a bit too high for us to recommend them as our favorite World Cup deal. That’s why we prefer cheaper streamer deals, which are plenty sufficient for viewers to catch all the matches without totally breaking the bank. That’ll be FOX One for English-language fans, and Peacock Premium for Spanish-language fans. Both are affordable, comprehensive options that are easy to cancel once the Cup’s up.

Related articles

  • How To Watch the 2026 FIFA World Cup in the US
  • Who Is Broadcasting the World Cup This Year?
  • 5 Ways To Watch the World Cup Without Cable

item.title: More Soccer Fans Choose DIRECTV Than Any Other Streamer
item.dc:creator: CableTV.com
item.description: According to our 2026 survey, 49% of soccer fans choose DIRECTV for live sports. Find out why it beats competitors like Fubo and YouTube TV.
item.content:encoded:
Soccer — or, let’s face it, fútbol — is not only the beautiful game. It’s considered the most popular sport in the world.

Soccer is also a huge reason why many DIRECTV customers signed up for the service.

Nearly 50% of people — 49% to be exact — chose DIRECTV specifically to watch live soccer, according to CableTV.com’s 2026 customer satisfaction survey. This eye-opening figure outpaces major live TV streaming competitors.

That data is a microcosm of how customers feel about DIRECTV as a whole. The company’s streaming option overtook YouTube TV for the first time in years in overall satisfaction. Specifically, 82% of survey respondents said they were either completely or very satisfied with DIRECTV.

With the 2026 FIFA World Cup just around the corner, it couldn’t be a better time to sign up for DIRECTV and feel that trust factor for yourself.

Here’s why fútbol fans favor DIRECTV

DIRECTV is a service enjoyed by millions of households around the country. With its robust offerings of national channels and regional sports networks, it’s no surprise that die-hard sports fans gravitate toward the platform.

But the actual user experience of watching DIRECTV, whether at home or on the go, is what sets it apart.

“The channel selection is solid, especially for sports and live events, and the picture quality is usually very good,” DIRECTV customer Lisa R. of Texas said in the survey. “The DVR features are easy to use, and I like that I can stream content on multiple devices when I’m not home.”

When the World Cup kicks off, those details will make all the difference in the viewing experience.

If you stream with DIRECTV, you get 4K playback on FOX broadcasts*. And with 104 total matches in a little over a month, you’ll definitely be using part of the unlimited DVR storage**.

For soccer fans, the Sports Central interface is user-friendly and well-organized. It just takes a couple of scrolls to find every live or upcoming match. And there will certainly be a prominent World Cup tournament hub for a seamless watching experience. This dedicated space is also great for watching any other sports you’re interested in.

DIRECTV offers soccer coverage year-round

When the 2026 FIFA World Cup takes over from June 11 to July 19, it’ll spark five weeks of nonstop soccer at the highest level, on the biggest stage. FOX Sports and Telemundo Deportes channels are home to all the action on TV.

But your access to world-class soccer doesn’t have to stop there.

DIRECTV’s channel offerings include CBS, CBS Sports Network, NBC, USA Network, TUDN, and Univision. It just so happens that those networks feature soccer year-round, but keep in mind that some local channels aren’t available in select markets.

So while your favorite national team stars might no longer be competing on the world stage, you can still watch them week in and week out on their respective club teams. DIRECTV has access to elite domestic leagues across the globe, including the Premier League, La Liga, and Liga MX. Plus, you’ll get high-stakes coverage when European teams clash midweek in the UEFA Champions League.

These competitions are well-covered by DIRECTV’s included sports networks. You can tune in to Premier League matches on NBC and USA Network, catch the UEFA Champions League on CBS and CBS Sports Network, track Liga MX on TUDN and Univision, and watch La Liga matchups on ESPN networks.

Choose the right DIRECTV package to get your sports fix

You might be thinking of becoming a DIRECTV customer just in time for the World Cup. But a true soccer fan will want to watch matches far beyond the final whistles, considering the World Cup only comes around once every four years.

To keep the action going, sign up for one of DIRECTV’s comprehensive plans. Its Signature Packages, which range between $89.99 and $169.99 per month‡, offer a variety of channels for sports, movies, TV, news, and more. These plans also include streaming apps like Disney+ and Hulu when using a compatible device†.

If you don’t need all those channels — or want to pay a little less — a DIRECTV Genre Pack® might be the way to go. For instance, the MySports® pack offers all the essential channels you’ll need to watch soccer, NBA, NHL, and more. At $64.99 per month^, it also includes a subscription to ESPN Unlimited.

Spanish speakers will want to consider the MiEspañol® Genre Pack, which includes channels like ESPN Deportes and Telemundo (where available), as well as a subscription to ViX Premium with ads.

There’s a reason 49% of current customers chose DIRECTV specifically for live soccer. And there’s a reason 82% of people who took our survey rated it so highly.

DIRECTV scores every chance it gets. Come join the team.

Disclaimers

*4K TV & content req’d for 4K streaming, where available. Video quality defined by resolution.
**Data connection required. Recordings expire after 9 months. Restr’s apply.
†Must be 18+. This will not replace any existing Disney+, Hulu, ESPN, or Disney bundle subscription you may already have. All other accounts must be managed separately. You must remain on an eligible plan to retain your offer.
‡New approved residential customers only. Service renews monthly unless you cancel. Cancel anytime. Add’l fees may apply to non-qualified customers. Device subject to Equipment Lease agmt. Add’l fee(s) may apply if equip. not returned. Credit card req’d. Restr’s apply.
^New residential customers only. A valid card is required. Your card may be charged a temporary hold during trial period. After 5-day trial, service renews monthly unless canceled. Cancel anytime. Prices may vary through third parties. Restr’s apply.

item.title: DIRECTV Expands Telemundo Reach to 98% of Hispanic TV Homes Ahead of World Cup
item.dc:creator: CableTV.com
item.description: DIRECTV’s Telemundo expansion now reaches 98% of U.S. Hispanic TV homes. Learn how to stream the 2026 FIFA World Cup with the MiEspañol Genre Pack.
item.content:encoded:
If you’ve been pining for more golazos in your life, but lived in an area where Telemundo wasn’t available, you’ll be happy to know that those days are over just in time for the FIFA World Cup, which kicks off on June 11. DIRECTV recently expanded access to its MiEspañol® Genre Pack®, which features more than 60 Spanish-language channels for $34.99 per month (plus tax). The flagship network, Telemundo, and its affiliates are now available in more than 80 additional markets across the country for the first time*. As a result, Telemundo and every other channel in the MiEspañol Genre Pack can serve about 98% of Hispanic TV homes in the United States. The expansion puts the channel and its local programming in more than 200 markets nationwide**.

Expanded ability to watch the World Cup

Telemundo is the official Spanish-language broadcaster of the 2026 FIFA World Cup. As soccer’s best players battle it out across the 11 U.S. host cities, many DIRECTV customers will now be able to watch and stream local Telemundo broadcasts for the very first time. Before the expansion, Telemundo affiliates were only available to DIRECTV customers in 20 major cities. The network’s national feed was available in 94 other metropolitan regions, but lacked localized programming. Expanding Telemundo’s local footprint ensures a more neighborly viewing experience. For MiEspañol subscribers, some of the markets now receiving their local Telemundo broadcast include Austin, Charlotte, Milwaukee, Nashville, New Orleans, Portland, and Reno. “With so many local station affiliates now joining the lineup, the experience becomes more localized, relevant, and widely available,” DIRECTV noted in its press release.

MiEspañol Genre Pack features

DIRECTV’s MiEspañol Genre Pack includes more than just Telemundo. It offers programming and content for fans of all sorts of entertainment — from live sports to movies and TV shows in Spanish. Of the 60+ channels, some highlights include: ESPN Deportes, Cinelatino, CNN en Español, History en Español, UniMás, Univision, TUDN, and TV Chile. The Genre Pack also includes a subscription to ViX Premium (with ads)†. The streaming service features original content, scripted television, and on-demand movies. For soccer fans, ViX offers thousands of live matches throughout the year, including coverage of the UEFA Champions League, Liga MX, and international friendlies. If you want to give the MiEspañol Genre Pack a whirl before you commit, DIRECTV offers a no-risk five-day free trial.

Inside the 2026 FIFA World Cup

As one of 2026’s biggest sporting events, the FIFA World Cup introduces an expanded format to the world’s most important soccer tournament. Fans can expect five weeks of nonstop action and intrigue, quests for bragging rights and national pride, and (occasional) flopping. The World Cup will feature 104 matches contested by 48 nations. The host countries for the 2026 tournament are the United States, Mexico, and Canada, with matches taking place in 16 host cities. Those cities include Boston, Los Angeles, Mexico City, and Toronto. A staple of the tournament will be Andrés Cantor, perhaps the most recognizable commentator in the sport. He’s the lead play-by-play announcer for Telemundo Deportes, which makes being a soccer fan and DIRECTV customer even more exhilarating during the tournament. Cantor’s presence is sure to be a major reason Spanish-speaking fans will tune in. But his passion can definitely entice English speakers as well, as it has for decades.

How to watch the World Cup with DIRECTV

DIRECTV really is your one-stop shop for the World Cup. Every match will air across Telemundo (92 matches) and Universo (12 matches). Each channel will simulcast matches on Peacock Premium, which is available as a standalone add-on or included with DIRECTV’s MOVIES EXTRA PACK. With Peacock Premium alone, you’ll be able to watch as many World Cup matches in Spanish as your eyes and ears can handle. And if you’re an English speaker or bilingual, DIRECTV carries FOX and FS1, the official English-language broadcast partners of the World Cup. FOX Sports released its full list of World Cup studio analysts, and it’s a who’s who of soccer royalty. Fans will see the likes of Alexi Lalas, Carli Lloyd, Landon Donovan, Thierry Henry, Zlatan Ibrahimovic, Rebecca Lowe, and more. Meanwhile, the elite talent calling games on Telemundo alongside Cantor will include Omar Zerón, José Luis López Salido, Jorge Calvo, Luis Omar Tapia, and Diego Balado.Disclaimers

*Local channels vary by market. **Channel lineup varies by market and is subject to change. †Access ViX through the ViX app or at https://vix.com. DIRECTV account verification required. No separate ViX registration needed. New residential customers only. A valid card is required. Your card may be charged a temporary hold during trial period. After the trial, service renews monthly unless canceled. Cancel anytime. Prices may vary through third parties. Restr’s apply. Visit directv.com/affiliates/genre-packs for more information.

item.title: No ‘Doctor Who’ Christmas Special This Year, As Russell T. Davies Exits Production
item.dc:creator: CableTV.com
item.description: It’s no secret that ‘Doctor Who’ is undergoing a major overhaul behind-the-scenes. But a new announcement confirms that we won’t be seeing the Doctor this Christmas.
item.content:encoded:
Sorry ‘Whovians,’ no holiday special this year

The BBC disappointed “Doctor Who” fans worldwide this morning when it announced that the previously confirmed Christmas special had been canceled. And what’s more—when the show does eventually return, it’ll be with a whole new creative team. Showrunner Russell T. Davies won’t be at the helm, and neither will production company Bad Wolf. As Davies posted on his Instagram account, “And so GOODBYE from me to Doctor Who but HELLO to a big new future for the show, as the BBC announces it’s putting the show out to tender. As a result, there won’t be a Christmas Special (…) there’s no need for it.” Davies goes on to explain that the BBC is sacrificing this year’s Christmas special in the hopes of a new, full season down the road. We already knew that something was up with the upcoming, hypothetical season of “Doctor Who.” Disney pulled its funding pretty abruptly after its second season, and the uncertainties surrounding the show’s future caused Ncuti Gatwa to leave the show and pursue other opportunities. Plus, there hasn’t been any indication that the BBC was actually filming the Christmas special it promised. This left the BBC to fund the show on its own without a clear plan of who the next Doctor would even be—Gatwa’s Doctor regenerated into former companion Rose Tyler (Billie Piper) at the end of his run, but fans are split on whether that’s even meant to be a permanent thing or a fun gimmick for a Christmas special. In his Instagram announcement, Davies confirmed that “no actor was ever approached to play the next Doctor,” which seems to point towards the latter. But with the 2026 Christmas special’s cancellation, it seems that we won’t be getting that gimmicky, fun Christmas adventure after all. We’ll just have to wait and see if the BBC can put together a new team that can live up to the show’s 60+ year history—and secure enough funding to pull it off.

Has ‘Doctor Who’ ever missed a Christmas special before?

While the show’s delayed production schedule isn’t too much of a surprise, it’s still a bummer to see another Christmas go by without a “Doctor Who” holiday special. The tradition started in 2005, when the Christmas special served as David Tennant’s introduction as the 10th Doctor, and has continued almost every year since. Many families have grown to see the special as an integral part of their holiday traditions. The first time “Doctor Who” skipped a Christmas was in 2018, when the show was preparing for a soft reboot and the introduction of its first female Doctor. When it returned in 2019, it wasn’t for a Christmas special, but for a New Year’s special. This trend would continue in 2020, 2021, and 2022, before the show soft-rebooted again and returned with a Christmas special in 2023. Then the show shuddered to a halt as Disney backed out of funding production, and there was no 2025 special. As seen with the transition to a new Doctor in 2019, skipping one year between Doctors or creative teams isn’t too unusual. But skipping two holiday seasons in a row? That’s almost unheard of.

The last 21 years of ‘Doctor Who’ holiday specials

Swipe Left to See All →
YearDoctorChristmas special?New Year's special?
2005David Tennant✔—
2006David Tennant✔—
2007David Tennant✔—
2008David Tennant✔—
2009David Tennant✔—
2010Matt Smith✔—
2011Matt Smith✔—
2012Matt Smith✔—
2013Matt Smith✔—

One thing’s especially clear to me after making this chart: Ncuti Gatwa didn’t get nearly enough episodes before Disney pulled the plug.

Who we are

At CableTV.com, we’re not just entertainment experts—we’re also fans just like you. So while our first priority is reporting the best and most accurate information about TV providers and streaming services, we also love talking about our favorite shows and movies. So you can count on us to share our honest opinions and unbridled enthusiasm. For more on our process, check out our Editorial Policy.

More industry news for TV fans

  • Remembering Anthony Stewart Head
  • Full ‘Doctor Who’ Timeline
  • ‘Doctor Who’ Switches Streaming Services

item.title: Fubo and NBCU Resolved Their Dispute. Here Are the Details of the New Deal
item.dc:creator: CableTV.com
item.description: Fubo and NBCUniversal resolved their dispute on June 10 and reached a carriage agreement that brings back channels like NBC, all of NBC’s regional sports networks, Telemundo, and many others.
item.content:encoded:
The fight is over. Fubo and NBCUniversal resolved their dispute on June 10 and reached a carriage agreement that brings back channels like NBC, all of NBC’s regional sports networks, Telemundo, and many others. The Fubo-NBC deal marks the end of a dispute that lasted nearly six months and saw both companies exchange public jabs at one another. “We’re thrilled to announce the return of NBCUniversal networks to Fubo, given their robust portfolio of top-tier sports, entertainment and news,” Todd Mathers, executive vice president of content strategy and acquisition, said in a statement. “Our agreement with NBCUniversal underscores Fubo’s promise to bring consumers more programming, value and choice through multiple packaging options.” I broke down the news and what it means for sports fans. Keep reading to learn more.

Fubo and NBCU fought over channel lineups and Peacock integration

The dispute between Fubo and NBCU was multifaceted. Fubo was upset that NBCU wanted a multiyear agreement for its channels moving to Versant. But Fubo wanted to carry those channels for only one year. Fubo also said NBCU was “discriminating against” the company because it didn’t allow Peacock integration on its platform, and claimed services like Amazon Prime Video and YouTube TV did have this functionality. Another issue was Fubo claimed NBCU was asking that non-sports channels be included on the Fubo Sports plan. If that happened, Fubo said, it would’ve raised the cost of the service for subscribers. NBC didn’t publicly address these specific allegations, but said generally that it offered Fubo fair rates and the same terms other distributors have agreed to.

Fubo offered subscribers multiple discounts

As soon as news of the dispute hit, Fubo offered current customers a $15 credit if the matter lasted for an “extended period.” It didn’t take long for the company to make good on that promise, pushing the discount even further. On December 5, Fubo announced it reduced the monthly price of its Pro and Elite plans to $73.99 per month from $83.99. The company stated that the drop was directly related to NBCU “pulling their networks” from the service. “The reduced pricing reflects Fubo’s commitment to delivering a competitively-priced premium live TV streaming experience,” Fubo said. Originally, Fubo said its $15 monthly discount would be enough for customers to pay for a Peacock subscription, which offers many of the NBCU programming that left Fubo.

CableTV.com verdict: Fubo becomes whole again just in time for the FIFA World Cup

Fubo and NBCUniversal coming together at this time is a huge win, even if the timing is a little suspect. The FIFA World Cup starts on June 11, and the news about the resolution to this dispute comes out a day earlier. Talk about getting it done at the 11th hour. Nonetheless, Fubo, a streaming service originally created specifically for live soccer, being out of the mix during the biggest soccer tournament in the world would’ve been a huge problem. The company avoided that with this deal. Fans who want to watch the World Cup on Fubo now have access to Telemundo and Universo, the tournament’s official Spanish-language broadcasters. Pair that with your local FOX station and FS1, and the full slate of channels and experiences is once again at your fingertips.

Related articles

    • Fubo Channel Lineup Guide
    • Fubo Pricing Explained
    • Peacock Review: Is It Worth the Price?

item.title: Is ‘Game of Thrones’ Good? The Haters Get Our Pound of Flesh and Then Some.
item.dc:creator: CableTV.com
item.description: Is Game of Thrones good? Our TV critics weigh in on the salacious show, along with popular opinions from fans and haters, 15 years later.
item.content:encoded:
“Game of Thrones” rated, 15 years later

When I first got my assignment to watch the first two seasons of “House of the Dragon” ahead of Season 3, not gonna lie, I was a little annoyed. You see, I’m one of the few, the proud, the “Game of Thrones” haters. Let me take a step back: I assigned myself “House of the Dragon” because my staff doesn’t love “Game of Thrones” either, and they didn’t want to (re)watch it. So we’re a crew of haters, but alas, what follows is an earnest attempt to determine if “Game of Thrones” is worth watching.

Don’t suffer impish internet speeds

Does your stream buffer and drop? Enter your zip code below to see faster, more reliable internet and TV providers in your area.

Critics weigh in on “Game of Thrones”

Per a recent CableTV.com data analysis, we ranked “Game of Thrones” the No. 7 TV show of all time, surpassed only by prestige TV shows like “The Sopranos” and a classic comedy, “Seinfeld.” We came to that very good ranking impartially, comparing scores from IMDb (93 points), Rolling Stone (89 points), and Variety (80 points) using the Borda method. Basically, the top show, “Breaking Bad,” scored 100 and is considered the top-rated TV show of all time. And then there’s Rotten Tomatoes, which also influenced the trade publications above. “Game of Thrones,” as of writing, scores 89%, with “top critics” rating it even higher at 96%, on the Tomatometer. Not too shabby! I think it’s safe to say that most TV critics think “Game of Thrones” is good, if not excellent, even if the critics here at CableTV.com couldn’t care less about the classic prestige fantasy drama. Given the mountain of data in favor of “Game of Thrones” being good, I had to revisit my longstanding refusal to even watch it. Enter the spinoff series “House of the Dragon.”

“Game of Thrones” viewers are kinda split, but not really

Much to my dismay, “House of the Dragon” served as a gateway drug to the real TV crackrock that is “Game of Thrones.” After three episodes of the GoT prequel, I was thinking, “Hey, this is pretty good.” Still, I didn’t want to commit to eight seasons of “Game of Thrones” based on “House of the Dragon” outperforming my expectations, so I reviewed some data from a recent CableTV.com fan survey. Spoiler alert: A lot of fans (i.e., not critics) think GoT is overrated. In fact, it placed No. 1 on our fan list of most overrated shows. Woof! Well, “Maybe I shouldn’t waste my time,” I thought. But then I remembered we ran another, very similar fan survey about the most iconic TV shows of all time. Unfortunately, for me, “Game of Thrones” ranked No. 1 in that survey, too. “What in the Seven Hells?!” Turns out a TV show can be divisive. Who knew? According to Rotten Tomatoes, 85% of fans gave GoT a rating of 3.5 stars or higher. In high-school-grading terms, that’s at least a C- from a large percentage of viewers. Fans tend to be hard on GoT.

The CableTV.com verdict on “Game of Thrones”

So, is “Game of Thrones” even good? After crunching the numbers and watching the entire franchise front-to-back (yes, even the prequels “House of the Dragon” and “A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms”), I can definitively say that “Game of Thrones” is good. Call me a psycho or a masochist, but the moment I realized it was fantastic was when a certain lord pushed a certain lady through a certain “moon” window. “Damn,” I thought, “This show is savage.” That said, I think it’s also fair to say that GoT ain’t for everyone. If you’re sensitive to gore, swearing, graphic sex, or incest, you’re probably not gonna enjoy bingeing it. And even if you’re down with the disgusting, you should probably know that fans kinda hated the last two seasons, so prepare for at least a little disappointment. For the rest of you fence sitters, what are you waiting for? You can binge the entire GoT franchise on HBO Max.

What to binge next

  • Shows Like “Game of Thrones”
  • Spiciest Episodes of “House of the Dragon”
  • What to Watch on HBO Max

item.title: BET+ is Shutting Down This Month: Everything Subscribers Need To Know
item.dc:creator: CableTV.com
item.description: BET+ is shutting down as a standalone streaming service and becoming a hub on Paramount+, much like SHOWTIME did a few years ago. Read on for more info.
item.content:encoded:
BET+ content is moving to Paramount+

As confirmed by Deadline’s reporting back in March, the BET+ streaming service is shutting down this month. As of June 1, over 1,000 hours of BET+ content have moved over to Paramount+ ahead of the BET+ closure. And all new BET content will go directly to Paramount+. BET and BET+ shows are available through both of Paramount+’s subscription tiers, so if you’re a Paramount+ subscriber, you’ll automatically get access to the BET content library. As of writing, there is no plan for this move to make Paramount+ any more expensive, although that could change once the transition is finalized. It’s also unclear exactly which day this month that BET+ will shut down, so if you want to catch up on your favorite episodes of Tyler Perry shows without an additional charge, do so now. Want to know more about how this will affect current BET+ subscribers? Read on.

BET+ is no longer accepting new subscribers

The BET+ website confirmed this month that “new BET+ subscriptions are no longer available.” However, existing subscribers are still able to sign in for the time being. As we reported back in March, existing BET+ subscribers will get the option to transfer their subscription over to Paramount+ with an exclusive discount. But their subscriptions won’t transfer automatically—unfortunately, continuing to watch BET content on demand means paying a higher subscription price. BET+ previously cost $5.99/mo., and the cheapest Paramount+ tier costs $8.99/mo. It is unclear how much of a discount BET+ subscribers are receiving, or how long the promo period will last. Keep an eye on CableTV.com’s news coverage, and we’ll let you know when we find out more. Also, it’s important to note that while BET+ is shutting down, the BET television network is not. So if you only watch BET content through a cable or satellite provider, you will remain unaffected.

Who we are

At CableTV.com, we’re not just entertainment experts—we’re also fans just like you. So while our first priority is reporting the best and most accurate information about TV providers and streaming services, we also love talking about our favorite shows and movies. So you can count on us to share our honest opinions and unbridled enthusiasm. For more on our process, check out our Editorial Policy.

More industry news for TV fans

  • The Cost of the World Cup
  • K-Manga Titles Come to Crunchyroll
  • Disney+ Is Planning to Kill Hulu

item.title: DIRECTV Streaming vs. DIRECTV Satellite: Live Streaming or Satellite TV Service – Which Is Better For You?
item.dc:creator: CableTV.com
item.description: DIRECTV’s streaming and satellite products both have some excellent features, but they’re both way too expensive to be worth it for most folks. Forced to choose one over the other, we’d pick DIRECTV’s streaming plans since they’re the most flexible (no contract means an easier escape) and avoid DIRECTV’s ridiculous second-year price hike.
item.content:encoded:

No self-installation

Data effective as of post date. Not all offers are available in all areas. See full disclaimer for details.

Status: Verified on 6/8/26

Is DIRECTV streaming or DIRECTV satellite better?

When it comes to DIRECTV, I have years of experience using both the classic satellite dish setup and its streaming form. Which one is better depends on your preferences. Each DIRECTV service has (nearly) identical high prices, two-year term agreements, and hidden fees that always seem to pop up. Also, both services are excellent for sports. The biggest differences are channel lineups, delivery method (satellite vs. streaming), devices (Genie or Gemini or Gemini Air) for live TV channels, movies, and shows. I’ve enjoyed both feature-rich services, but if you forced me to choose, I’d go with DIRECTV’s streaming plans. It’s just simpler to install and use—and cancel without a penalty. However, my overall recommendation is that DIRECTV plans change too frequently and its prices are much too high; you can often get more bang for your buck elsewhere. Check out YouTube TV or Hulu + Live TV or, if you’re still considering a satellite setup, DISH. But maybe you’re not yet ready to turn your back on DIRECTV—perhaps because you want to access the services’ top-shelf sportsball experience. If so, read on for a deeper comparison of both services.

Don't be caught offsides with the wrong streamer this World Cup

DIRECTV is the streaming service hooligans choose for football. According to CableTV.com's 2026 customer satisfaction survey, more fans (49%) watch soccer on DIRECTV than any other streaming service. That's more than rivals Fubo and YouTube TV. What's more, a staggering 97% of DIRECTV streaming subscribers reported satisfaction with their live sports experience. So, what are you waiting for? Hit the World Cup pitch this summer by streaming it on DIRECTV.

View DIRECTV Plans

What else is out there?

You can get streaming TV services wherever you can get a steady internet connection. Satellite TV services like DISH and DIRECTV are available almost anywhere. But cable TV providers’ availability depends on your location. Enter your zip code below to find providers in your area.

Compare DIRECTV streaming and satellite plans head-to-head

Provider Price* Connection type Channel count DVR storage Simultaneous recordings Details
DIRECTV (streaming)
Best for flexibility 		$59.99–$129.99/mo. for 1st mo. + taxes & fees Internet 90–185+ Unlimited for $10.00/mo. 5 View Plans for DIRECTV STREAM

Read DIRECTV Streaming Review
DIRECTV (satellite)
Best for DVR and features 		$89.99–$169.99/mo. for 24 mos. plus taxes & fees Satellite 165–340+ 200 hrs. (Genie) or 450 hrs. (Gemini) for $10.00/mo. 3 View Plans for DIRECTV

Read DIRECTV Satellite Review

Data effective as of post date. Not all offers are available in all areas. See full disclaimer for details.

Let’s recap the similarities and differences between DIRECTV’s streaming and satellite plans. DIRECTV’s streaming plans have 90–185+ channels—that’s a good bit less than its satellite plans. That’s not such a big deal because the lineups are curated to include mostly popular channels—you won’t get as many filler channels as you find on DIRECTV. DIRECTV’s streaming plans connect to your home Wi-Fi network and deliver your channels via either the Gemini Air device (first one’s free), or one of several streaming devices that support the DIRECTV app.Pro tip: If DIRECTV’s traditional plans are too expensive, but you like the brand, check out DIRECTV’s Genre Packs. These themed packages are dedicated to entertainment, kids, news, and sports content, featuring smaller price tags. They also have fewer channels—but more of the ones you want—and some even include streaming services like Disney+, ESPN Select, Hulu, and HBO Max.

If satellite TV is your only option, we think DIRECTV is the best in the biz. Still, some folks love DISH Network, which has slightly fewer channels than DIRECTV, but almost as many sports networks and add-ons. DISH also has the more powerful Hopper 3 DVR and similarly guarantees your monthly payment for two years. However, as I mentioned, I steer people away from DIRECTV and towards live TV streaming services like YouTube TV or Hulu + Live TV. While they have fewer channels than DIRECTV, I’ve found they offer better overall value with better affordability, no hidden fees, curated lineups of popular channels, and free unlimited cloud DVR storage.

DIRECTV streaming vs. satellite prices and plans: Both are expensive

DIRECTV’s streaming and satellite services each have four plans with similar monthly payments—but wildly divergent channel counts. Let’s see how they rate in terms of overall value.

DIRECTV streaming pricing and plans

Package Price* Channel count Popular channels Details
ENTERTAINMENT $59.99/mo. for 1st month + taxes & fees 90+ AMC, CNN, ESPN, Hallmark Channel, HGTV, MSNBC, Nickelodeon, and TLC View Plan
CHOICE™ $64.99/mo. for 1st month + taxes & fees 125+ Bravo, Disney, IFC, Nick Jr., OWN, Science, Travel Channel, and UniMás View Plan
ULTIMATE $89.99/mo. for 1st month + taxes & fees 160+ Magnolia Network, Golf Channel, LOGO, Nat Geo WILD, NHL Network, and Universal Kids View Plan
PREMIER $129.99/mo. for 1st month + taxes & fees 185+ CINEMAX®, STARZ®, Paramount+ Premium and HBO MaxTM View Plan

Data effective as of post date. Not all offers are available in all areas. See full disclaimer for details.

All live TV streaming services, not just DIRECTV, have fewer channels than cable and satellite TV services. So it’s not like DIRECTV is trying to give you less for your money. It’s how live TV streaming services roll—they offer quality over quantity or more popular channels instead of filler networks. The streaming CHOICE plan with 125+ channels is the best value. With CHOICE and ULTIMATE packages, new customers get three free months of CINEMAX, MGM+, HBO Max, Paramount+ Premium, and STARZ. But if you go with PREMIER, those premium channels are a permanent part of your plan.

See All DIRECTV Streaming Channels

DIRECTV satellite pricing and plans

Package Price* Channel count Popular channels Details
ENTERTAINMENT $89.99/mo. for 24 mos. plus taxes and fees 175+ AMC, CNN, ESPN, Hallmark Channel, HGTV, MSNBC, Nickelodeon, and TLC View Plan
CHOICE™ $94.99/mo. for 24 mos. plus taxes and fees 215+ Bravo, Disney, IFC, Nick Jr., OWN, Science, Travel Channel, and UniMás View Plan
ULTIMATE $124.99/mo. for 24 mos. plus taxes and fees 270+ Magnolia Network, Golf Channel, LOGO, Nat Geo WILD, NHL Network, and Universal Kids View Plan
PREMIER™ $169.99/mo. for 24 mos. plus taxes and fees 350+ CINEMAX, STARZ, Paramount+ Premium, and HBO Max View Plan

Data effective as of post date. Not all offers are available in all areas. See full disclaimer for details.

DIRECTV’s satellite and streaming packages are essentially the same in terms of popular channels. The difference is that the streaming packages cut out the filler channels you’ll probably never watch. Both providers now charge close to the same price for their plans—but the channel counts are vastly different, as I’ve shown.

See All DIRECTV Satellite Channels
Pro tip: Check out our guide to the Hidden Costs of DIRECTV, which explains all of the service’s various hidden charges, including fees for activation, devices, early cancellation, late payments, local channels, and more.

DIRECTV streaming and satellite plans have different equipment and DVR storage

When it comes to DVRs, the DIRECTV Genie (200 hours) and Genie 2 (450 hours) are powerful units with limited streaming app support. That said, the DIRECTV Gemini (450–unlimited hours) and Gemini Air (unlimited hours) aren’t as feature-rich as the Genies, but their accompanying cloud DVR storage is much greater. Check out each device’s vital statistics below.

DIRECTV streaming vs. satellite DVR

DVR Price Storage Simultaneous recordings Video quality Streaming app support Details
DIRECTV Gemini Air (optional) $10.00/mo. Unlimited (cloud) 3 Up to 4K Disney+, ESPN, Hulu, HBO Max, Paramount+, Peacock View Plans for DIRECTV
DIRECTV Genie $10.00/mo. 200 hrs. (physical) 5 Up to 4K HBO Max, Netflix, Pandora View Plans for DIRECTV
DIRECTV Genie 2 (wireless) $10.00/mo. 450 hrs. (cloud) 7 Up to 4K HBO Max, Netflix, Pandora View Plans for DIRECTV
DIRECTV Gemini $10.00/mo. 450 hrs.–unlimited (cloud) 3 Up to 4K Disney+, ESPN, Hulu, HBO Max, Netflix, Paramount+, Peacock, Prime Video View Plans for DIRECTV

Data effective as of post date. Not all offers are available in all areas. See full disclaimer for details.

DIRECTV Gemini Air and cloud DVR

When you sign up for DIRECTV’s streaming plans, you can choose to use the service’s new Gemini Air device ($10.00 per month), which includes a universal voice remote, support for most major streaming apps, and unlimited DVR. Or you can save ten bucks a month by using one of the many streaming devices, smartphones, or tablets that you probably already own—and already support the DIRECTV app. Unlimited cloud DVR has a nine-month keep time for recordings, and that sure beats DIRECTV’s old cloud DVR offer: A measly 20 hours free or unlimited storage for $10.00 a month, with only a 90-day keep time. It’s also infinitely more DVR storage than you get with the Genies.

DIRECTV Genie DVRs and wireless Gemini

If you like to keep movies, shows, and sporting events to watch at your leisure, the best option is DIRECTV’s Genie (200 hours, five simultaneous recordings) or wireless Genie 2 (450 hours, three simultaneous recordings). The Genie DVRs don’t auto-delete your recordings unless you’re out of storage.

The basic Genie (wired) has enough storage and tuners for most users. The Genie 2 is best for committed couch creatures and their binge-watching habits, and the unit is wireless so you can avoid cable clutter. Another wireless option is the Gemini (different from the Gemini Air), which includes a universal voice remote, streaming app support, and 450 hours of cloud DVR storage. If you have multiple TVs, you can add DIRECTV on up to seven more screens in your home with additional Genie or Gemini devices ($10.00 a month each). To learn more about DIRECTV whole-home DVR, check out our DIRECTV Genie review.

DIRECTV satellite vs. streaming installation = simple vs. complicated

Installing and setting up DIRECTV’s streaming plans is a breeze. In fewer than five minutes, I downloaded the app to my Roku Ultra and smartphone, signed in, and started browsing what to watch while also building my watchlist. (Note: We haven’t tested the Gemini Air device. When we do, we’ll update this article. For now, it’s safe to assume the setup process will be just as easy.) The installation process is much more complicated, and potentially frustrating, with DIRECTV’s satellite service. It required setting up an install appointment where a technician installed the dish on my roof, drilled cable holes into my house, and set up my Genie DVR. That involved taking time off work and took significantly longer to complete. Also, apartment dwellers may need to have their landlord sign an installation approval form. On the bright side, installation is free of charge.

DIRECTV streaming vs. satellite: Two services, one streaming app

Comparing DIRECTV’s apps is a little tricky. Allow us to elaborate.

DIRECTV streaming app

All streaming services are based around an app that you use on a compatible device like a Chromecast, gaming console, personal computer, smart TV, smartphone, streaming stick, or tablet. So, while the DIRECTV app is an extra way to enjoy your DIRECTV satellite package on the go, it’s the main way to use the streaming service. When I put the DIRECTV app to the test, I quickly understood why it boasts such high ratings—4.6 out of 5.0 on both Google Play and the Apple App Store. From my perspective, the app consistently delivered an easy, intuitive user experience across every platform I tried. One of the first things that struck me was the app’s incredible device compatibility. I found it worked seamlessly with my Android and iOS devices, Amazon Fire TV sticks, Apple TV, Chromecast, NVIDIA Shield, and even my Roku. I also experimented with it on my Google and Roku smart TVs, as well as various internet browsers, and had no issues whatsoever. It’s safe to say you probably already own something that works with it. Now, on the downside, I was a bit disappointed to discover there’s no gaming console support. As a gamer, that’s a feature I’d love to see in the future. But I was pleasantly surprised to find that the app is compatible with the Apple Vision Pro virtual reality device and even select Peloton models, which is a nice touch. The DIRECTV device compatibility page offers all the nitty-gritty details like specific model numbers and operating system requirements.

DIRECTV streaming vs. DIRECTV satellite FAQ

Why you should trust us

At CableTV.com, we constantly analyze prices, plans, and specifications of TV providers like DIRECTV so our readers can make informed buying decisions. We’ve also tested DIRECTV streaming and DIRECTV satellite from signup through activation, and surveyed other customers to see what they thought about providers. To learn more about our process, see our How We Rank page.

Related articles

    • DIRECTV Streaming Review
    • DIRECTV Satellite Review
    • Best TV Providers
Disclaimer

CHOICE: ($124.98/mo. w/req’d $10/mo. TV Access Fee & Regional Sports Fee of up to $19.99/mo.) w/ 24-mo.agmt. Autopay and paperless bill req’d. One-time $49.95 activation fee is extra and applies. PREMIER™: ($199.98/mo. w/req’d $10/mo. TV Access Fee & Regional Sports Fee of up to $19.99/mo.) w/ 24-mo. agmt. Autopay and paperless bill req’d. One-time $49.95 activation fee is extra and applies.

Streaming: Entertainment: for 1st month + taxes & fees (Up to $69.99/mo. w/ req’d fees. Price reflects $30 off for 1st mo.; $10 off for mos. 2-24. Cancel anytime.) item.title: The 7 Spiciest Episodes of ‘House of the Dragon’: From the Cursed Wedding to That Impromptu Kiss
item.dc:creator: CableTV.com
item.description: Relive the fire, blood, and betrayals! Dive into our ranking of the seven spiciest ‘House of the Dragon’ episodes before Season 3 drops this June.
item.content:encoded:
The “Game of Thrones” prequel, “House of the Dragon,” has earned its reputation as an unhinged family drama, serving up incest, political betrayal, eye gouging, and orgiastic excess in nearly equal measure. Of course, the dragons are fiery (or spicy, if you will) and, reportedly, quite pungent. But the real heat comes from the Targaryens themselves, who treat every wedding, war council, and whispered hallway encounter as an opportunity for maximum chaos. Ahead of Season 3 this June, here are the spiciest episodes from both seasons of “House of the Dragon,” guaranteed to make you gasp, wince, and even cringe. Caution: spoilers ahead.

Level up your internet for streaming!

Enter your zip code below to see the top internet and TV providers in your neighborhood.

“The Heirs of the Dragon” (Season 1, Episode 1)

The series premiere goes balls out, literally. Prince Daemon Targaryen, Captain of the City Watch and professional chaos agent, leads a nocturnal sweep of King’s Landing that results in dismemberments across the board — limbs, heads, and, most memorably, an on-screen shart followed by what appear to be a pair of severed testicles. The carnage is only interrupted by Daemon’s failed after-hours rendezvous with Mysaria, a madam he’s been seeing on the side. But the scene in question ends in sad-boy sulking rather than sexual satisfaction. And if that weren’t spicy enough, tourney violence adds another layer of grime, with skulls crushed on camera and blood spurting everywhere. Then the episode delivers its gut-punch finale: an emergency C-section performed without anesthesia, blood soaking the king’s robes, followed by the deaths of both the queen and the male heir to The Realm.

“King of the Narrow Sea” (Season 1, Episode 4)

Caution: Grooming is always disgusting and upsetting, so you might want to skip this one. After three episodes of familial flirting, Prince Daemon lures his niece Rhaenyra through a secret passage via handwritten note, takes her on a hand-holding stroll through King’s Landing, and gets her thoroughly drunk before steering her into a house of prostitution packed with tattooed dancers and lots of group sex. In the middle of all this commotion, Daemon whispers to Rhaenyra something to the effect of, “Hey, girl, hey — follow your heart.” Then he proceeds to kiss her passionately and undo her tunic before reconsidering the entire enterprise and fleeing into the night, resulting in incestum interruptus. Back at the castle, Rhaenyra redirects her energy toward Ser Criston Cole, her sworn protector, in a fully nude encounter that runs the whole gamut of sexual positions. Disturbing yet erotic, “King of the Narrow Sea” is the definitive tour through the seediest corners of King’s Landing, IMO.

“We Light the Way” (Season 1, Episode 5)

Not all spice happens in the bedroom. Episode 5 earns its place on this list because the royal wedding of Rhaenyra and her cousin Prince Laenor Velaryon becomes a pressure cooker of suppressed fury and bruised egos. The rage boils over when Criston and Ser Joffrey Lonmouth come to blows in a face-smashing, fatal brawl after Joffrey divulges to Criston that he knows Rhaenyra — his lover’s beard — slept with Criston. Meanwhile, Daemon and Rhaenyra embrace in yet another simmering, tension-filled confrontation, but this time in public, on the dance floor, which is bold even by their standards. And to top it all off, King Viserys collapses, stricken with an unknown malady. Not nearly as bloody as the infamous Red Wedding from “Game of Thrones” Season 3, what some critics are calling the Cursed Wedding, adds layers of drama and courtly complications to a classic GoT plot send-up.

“Driftmark” (Season 1, Episode 7)

Episode 7 finally breaks the tension that’s been building between Daemon and Rhaenyra since Episode 1. The scene itself is as underwhelming as it is gross: Scored to a celestial melody — an interesting creative choice — Rhaenyra stares into the middle distance and strokes her uncle’s back. Incestum completum. Elsewhere in the episode, a young Prince Aemond Targaryen finally finds his dragon, Vhagar, fully grown and lying on the beach like a very large, fire-breathing Labrador retriever. That heartwarming moment is immediately countered by a violent scuffle among cousins that results in the loss of Aemond’s eye and Queen Alicent Hightower slashing Rhaenyra in retaliation. In the wake of Joffrey’s death, Laenor’s death is staged by his new lover and sworn guard, Ser Qarl Correy, allowing Daemon and Rhaenyra to marry in a hasty ceremony sealed in “pure” Targaryen blood while Laenor and Qarl flee Westeros as lovers.

“Rhaenyra the Cruel” (Season 2, Episode 2)

Season 2 of “House of the Dragon” tones down the spice a bit, but it still brings the heat in “Rhaenyra the Cruel,” in which simmering family tensions finally erupt into something genuinely ugly and intimate. The episode’s most shocking sequence involves twin brothers, Ser Arryk and Ser Erryk Cargyll (the most confusing of all the confusing names, LOL), fighting each other, with Erryk slaying his beloved brother and then falling on his own sword out of guilt. On the other end of the tonal spectrum, Queen Alicent and Criston’s secret, loathing-fueled physical relationship gets slappy, taking the series into kink territory. Even Mysaria, the madam from Season 1, resurfaces as a pressure point, solidifying her political power in the Targaryen court that eventually leads to some more spicy shenanigans later in the season. (Don’t worry, we’ll get into it.) The episode is proof that the Dance of the Dragons isn’t just about battlefield maneuvers. It’s about how war corrodes everyone it touches, including the people who thought they were safely on the sidelines.

“Smallfolk” (Season 2, Episode 6)

The episode “Smallfolk” delivers on its implied premise that common folk (i.e., non-royalty or landed gentry) can have a massive impact on The Realm. In a nutshell, Rhaenyra starts recruiting the descendants of Targaryen bastards as dragon riders, theorizing that some of these distant relatives will have strong enough Targaryen blood to handle the dragons. But perhaps the most notable instance of smallfolk spice comes in the form of an impromptu kiss between Rhaenyra and Mysaria. As reported in Variety, Sonia Mizuno (aka Mysaria) explained that the kiss embodied the following romantic realization: “Oh yeah, of course. I’ve fancied that person for six months, and I didn’t realize.” The core emotions behind the kiss seem to be curiosity and longing, which arguably feel more spicy than classic GoT mainstays like salacious incest or full frontal. In either case, the actors have implied that this kiss will definitely affect the twists and turns of Season 3.

“The Queen Who Ever Was” (Season 2, Episode 8)

Season 2’s final episode brings in one of the most entertainingly outrageous characters the show has ever introduced: Admiral Sharako Lohar. In a move that is simultaneously practical and jaw-dropping, the polygamous lesbian tasks Lord Tyland Lannister with the job of impregnating her wives after mud wrestling him to a draw. The logistics are left tastefully ambiguous. The audacity is not. Needless to say, I hope to see a lot more of Lohar in Season 3.Elsewhere, the episode upends the established social order as bastard Targaryens — previously sidelined by their birth status — emerge as dragon masters, reshuffling the war’s power dynamics entirely. (What’s spicier than upended social hierarchies?) Aemond, never one for restraint, goes rogue and begins torching entire castles. This decision sidelines a hideously disfigured Aegon and signals that the endgame of the Dance of the Dragons is going to be very, very messy.

Spiciest “House of the Dragon” episodes FAQ

How many episodes does "House of the Dragon" have?

"House of the Dragon" has two completed seasons as of early 2026, with 10 episodes in Season 1 and 8 episodes in Season 2, for a total of 18 episodes. A third season of eight more episodes premieres in June 2026.

Where can I watch "House of the Dragon"?

"House of the Dragon" streams exclusively on HBO Max, and an HBO Max subscription is required to watch both seasons on demand.

Is "House of the Dragon" as graphic as "Game of Thrones"?

"House of the Dragon" has the same mature content rating as its predecessor, featuring graphic violence, sexual content, and strong language. Fans of the original series will find the tone and intensity immediately familiar.

Do I need to watch "Game of Thrones" before "House of the Dragon"?

No prior viewing of "Game of Thrones" is required to follow "House of the Dragon," though familiarity with the world of Westeros and Targaryen lore adds useful context, particularly around the significance of dragons and the Iron Throne.

Who are the main characters in "House of the Dragon"?

The series centers on Princess — later Queen — Rhaenyra Targaryen and her rivalry with Queen Alicent Hightower over succession to the Iron Throne, with Prince Daemon Targaryen serving as the show's most combustible wild card throughout both seasons.

What to binge next

  • Shows Like “Game of Thrones”
  • What To Watch on HBO Max
  • How To Watch “Outlander”
  • Royal Resumes: Game of Thrones Cast—Where Are They Now?

item.title: GFiber vs. AT&T Fiber: Comparing Internet Speeds, Pricing, and More In the Fiber Fight
item.dc:creator: CableTV.com
item.description: Deciding between GFiber or AT&T Fiber? Check out our head-to-head breakdown to see which fiber internet provider reigns supreme.
item.content:encoded:
Editor's choice
Editorial rating (4.00/5)

$70.00–$150.00/mo.

Download speeds up to 8 Gbps
Cheaper plans than AT&T
No budget-friendly internet plans

Read full review

Value-friendly internet plans
Great phone bundle deals

Read full review

Value-friendly internet plans
Great phone bundle deals
More expensive than GFiber

Is GFiber or AT&T Fiber better?

We recommend GFiber if performance and cost are your biggest internet concerns. Across the board, GFiber has cheaper plans and better customer service than AT&T, which is why we gave it our Editor’s Choice Award for best internet provider in the U.S. But if you’re shopping for an AT&T bundle or need a value-friendly internet setup, AT&T offers customers a deeper catalog of internet plans and bundle deals without tying you to an annual contract.Those sweet wireless bundles and fast speeds are a couple of the reasons why it won our Editor’s Choice Award for best no-contract internet. GFiber also topped AT&T in CableTV.com’s annual Internet Customer Satisfaction Survey. GFiber ranked third in overall satisfaction with a whopping 83% high approval rating. AT&T was no slouch with a seventh-place finish, but the provider had an average 74% high approval rating. Keep reading for a full head-to-head between GFiber and AT&T to see which provider comes out on top.

Why should you trust us? At CableTV.com, our internet experts have spent thousands of hours testing all of the best (and worst) internet services, and hundreds more hours tracking and researching industry trends. For this article, we drew from our existing GFiber and AT&T internet reviews. As part of these reviews, we surveyed hundreds of real GFiber and AT&T internet customers to see how they felt about their internet service. We also combined this data with our original research and reporting to see which internet provider comes out on top.

Ready to get fiber internet?

Enter your zip code below to see if AT&T Fiber or GFiber are available in your area.

Compare GFiber or AT&T Fiber to other providers


Customer Rating

Prices starting at
 $70.00/mo.

Max download speeds up to
 8,000 Mbps

Service Type
 Fiber

View Plansfor Google Fiber

Read Google Fiber Review


Customer Rating

Prices starting at
 $50.00/mo.

Max download speeds up to
 5,000 Mbps

Service Type
 Fiber

View Plansfor AT&T

Read AT&T Review

Pricing, speed and availability not guaranteed. Subject to change.

GFiber vs. AT&T Fiber plans and prices

As fiber internet service providers (ISPs), GFiber and AT&T Fiber share much in common. Both providers offer features like unlimited data and no-contract pricing. AT&T has cheaper fiber plans with lower download speeds, but GFiber gig-internet plans are cheaper than AT&T Fiber gig-internet plans. However, the biggest differences between the ISPs lie in their fine print. Read on to see what you need to know if you’re deciding between AT&T Fiber and GFiber.

GFiber plans and prices

Plan Price Download speed Upload speed
1 Gig $70.00/mo. 1,000Mbps 1,000Mbps
2 Gig $100.00/mo. 2,000Mbps 1,000Mbps
5 Gig* $125.00/mo. 5,000Mbps 5,000Mbps
8 Gig* $150.00/mo. 8,000Mbps 8,000Mbps

Data effective as of post date. Offers and availability may vary by location and are subject to change.

*Available in select markets only. Plus taxes and fees. Upload/download speed and device streaming claims are based on maximum wired speeds. Actual Internet speeds are not guaranteed and may vary based on factors such as hardware and software limitations, latency, packet loss, etc.

GFiber technically has four plans, but its 5 Gig and 8 Gig plans aren’t widely available yet—GFiber is gradually rolling out 5 Gig and 8 Gig services across its network. (And considering GFiber itself isn’t widely available either, consider yourself lucky if you’re in an area with GFiber and 5 Gig or 8 Gig.) Most GFiber customers will be limited to its 1 Gig or 2 Gig plans, although they’re miles better than competing cable internet plans. On cost alone, GFiber’s 1 Gig plan is more affordable compared to AT&T. You’ll be paying more monthly on AT&T, although GFiber’s upload speeds cap off at 1,000Mbps compared to AT&T’s symmetric transfer speeds. But GFiber’s starting plan is equivalent to a premium internet option for most households. 1 Gig offers excellent performance, but it’ll be overkill for homes with basic internet needs or budget concerns, and we wish GFiber offered a sub-$50 internet plan for these customers.

View Plansfor Google Fiber

AT&T Fiber plans and prices

Plan Price Download speed Upload speed
Internet 300 $50.00/mo. 300Mbps 300Mbps
Internet 500 $65.00/mo. 500Mbps 500Mbps
1 Gig Internet $65.00/mo.* 1,000Mbps 1,000Mbps
5 Gig Internet $125.00/mo. 5,000Mbps 5,000Mbps

Data effective as of post date. Offers and availability may vary by location and are subject to change. Above pricing is after $10/mo Autopay & Paperless bill discount with a debit card or bank account; if using a credit card, then price is $5/mo more. Pricing includes Equipment Fee. Up to $99 install fee may apply, plus tax where applicable.

*Price after discounts: $25/mo for new customers, and $10/mo w/ elig Autopay & Paperless bill. Monthly State Cost Recovery charge applies in NV, OH, TX. One time install fee may apply.

Internet 300 and Internet 500 are great starter internet plans for most households, while 1 Gig Internet has the same specifications as GFiber’s 1 Gig plan. As with GFiber, AT&T 5 Gig Internet is gradually being rolled out across AT&T’s fiber internet footprint, and availability varies depending on your location. As I said, AT&T’s 1 Gig plan is also a bit pricier than GFiber’s. Both providers’ 5 Gig plans are now priced the same, though, so speed demons can take their pick.

View Plansfor AT&T

What do real customers think of GFiber and AT&T Fiber?

As part of CableTV.com’s annual Internet Customer Satisfaction Survey, we talked to more than 7,000 real internet customers across the U.S. to see how they felt about their internet providers. GFiber’s third-place finish in overall satisfaction was powered by a few factors: it’s fast, has great customer service, and rarely goes down.

  • 87% of respondents felt GFiber was highly effective at meeting their needs.
  • 81% of customers would recommend GFiber to someone else.
  • But only 75% of customers felt that GFiber was competitively priced compared to other internet providers.

Because I trust Google, and my experience with GFiber was wonderful, comfortable, and completely convinced of it, even when I compare it with other companies, it is the best.

—GFiber customer in Chicago
AT&T’s seventh-place finish in overall satisfaction was nothing to sneeze at. But AT&T generally fell behind GFiber in areas including customer service reliability and price.

  • 74% of AT&T customers felt that AT&T fully met their expectations for home internet service.
  • But only 69% of customers were fully satisfied with AT&T customer service.
  • And 60% of respondents felt that AT&T’s prices were competitive compared to the competition.

haven't had any issues with AT&T. I used to have it all my other house, but transferred it to my new house. Biggest issue was they had issues transferring it and had to create a different account for it. My old account had my cell phone and internet combined and gave me a discount doing so.

—AT&T Fiber customer in Texas

How reliable is GFiber and AT&T internet?

GFiber AT&T Fiber
Average latency 23.4 milliseconds 15.3 milliseconds
Internet reliability satisfaction rating 83% positive 75% positive
Internet speed satisfaction rating 85% positive 75% positive
According to speed test data from our partners at HighSpeedInternet.com, AT&T holds a slight advantage over GFiber when it comes to average latency times. But GFiber lapped AT&T on our speed and reliability satisfaction polling—GFiber broke the 80% satisfaction mark on both questions, while AT&T landed at 75% on both metrics.

GFiber vs. AT&T Fiber customer service around the web: what do other people say?

Like a lot of split fiber/DSL internet companies, AT&T’s fiber side operates much more smoothly than its DSL services. In the 2024 edition of the American Customer Satisfaction Index (ACSI), AT&T Fiber had a first-place finish with a whopping 80% satisfaction score, while GFiber was right behind with a 76% positive rating. But AT&T’s DSL side continues to weigh the provider down, as it hit a 69% satisfaction score in the ACSI.

Availability: Can you get GFiber or AT&T Fiber in your neighborhood?

AT&T Fiber and GFiber are fairly close in service availability. GFiber is currently available in more than 20 cities across the U.S. AT&T Fiber is available across 21 states, but exact availability varies depending on your location.

  • Atlanta, GA
  • Austin, TX
  • Charlotte, NC
  • Chicago, IL
  • Denver, CO
  • Des Moines, IA
  • Huntsville, AL
  • Kansas City, KS
  • Kansas City, MO
  • Lakewood, CO
  • Mesa, AZ
  • Miami, FL
  • Nashville, TN
  • Oakland, CA

GFiber and AT&T Fiber specs and features: Gear, data, and more

Download speeds and pricing are a big part of any internet shopping decision, but they’re not the only factors worth considering. Here’s what else you get with AT&T Fiber and GFiber.

Free Wi-Fi equipment

Both AT&T Fiber and GFiber internet plans come with a free Wi-Fi gateway, which connects your home to the internet and works like a combo router/modem. GFiber utilizes a mesh Wi-Fi setup for full-home coverage. If your home isn’t set up for fiber internet, AT&T Fiber and GFiber will install an Optical Network Terminal (ONT) mounted to a wall inside.

GFiber and AT&T Fiber deals

GFiber doesn’t offer many deals, but sometimes you can get discounts on other Google services, like cloud storage. Check out GFiber’s website to see if you can dig up a deal or discount. AT&T regularly offers deals like free gift cards and discount codes for new fiber internet customers. Qualifying AT&T Wireless customers can also save $20 monthly on AT&T Fiber. Check out our AT&T deals guide for this month’s best AT&T deals.

Do GFiber and AT&T Fiber have fees or extra charges?

The good news is that both GFiber and AT&T Fiber don’t really have any hidden costs. Another reason why both providers won Editor’s Choice Awards this year.

GFiber fees and extra charges

Fees Amount
None None
As with most fiber ISPs, GFiber is fee-free—you won’t have to deal with hidden price increases after 12 months, data overage fees, installation fees, or equipment rental charges. Google has some penalty fees that are limited to issues like equipment replacements.

AT&T Fiber fees and extra charges

Fees Amount
Installation support Up to $99
AT&T has also streamlined its fee structure to simplify things for fiber internet customers. AT&T Fiber has no contract, equipment, or data overage fees, so you’ll get straightforward monthly pricing. AT&T also offers free installation, but you may have to pay an additional fee if you initially decline and later request technical support.

Unlimited data

GFiber and AT&T Fiber include unlimited data with each of their plans, with no caps on either. You’ll be able to stream, download, and upload content with no overage fees, ever. For context, we don’t give any of our Editor’s Choice Awards to ISPs that ding you for data overages.

Phone and internet bundles

You can add VoIP (voice over internet protocol) home phone service to either AT&T Fiber or GFiber internet plans for an additional monthly fee. GFiber’s single home phone is a flat $10 a month, whereas AT&T offers several bundle plans starting at $24.99, as well as AT&T Wireless mobile plans. We give AT&T the edge here since you can score a bundle discount when you add a wireless plan. Who needs VoIP anyway?

GFiber vs. AT&T Fiber FAQ

Why you should trust us

We drew from resources including our annual customer satisfaction survey and our research around GFiber and AT&T Fiber. We then analyzed this data to compare both companies head to head and find out which provider offers the best internet service for new customers. For more on our process, see our How We Rank page.

  • GFiber Review
  • Best Fiber Internet Providers
